name: Valais (Switzerland)
description: A square flag divided vertically white and red, charged with thirteen five-pointed stars counterchanged against the two halves.
history: The red-and-white war flag of the Bishop of Sion dates to about 1220; stars for the districts (Dixains) were added from 1613, growing from six to thirteen when Conthey joined as Valais entered the restored Confederation in 1815.

features.role: thirteen stars, one per district (Dixain), counterchanged red on white and white on red
